evals.prompt:
Hey can you review my resume? I'm targeting Staff PM roles at AI-first B2B SaaS companies (Glean, Notion, Linear, Superhuman type of companies). My main concern is that my bullets might be too feature-focused and not outcome-focused enough. Here's the full thing:

---

JORDAN MILLER
jordan.miller@gmail.com | linkedin.com/in/jordanmiller | Boston, MA

SUMMARY
Experienced Product Manager with 8 years of experience building B2B SaaS products. Skilled in agile methodologies, cross-functional collaboration, and product strategy.

EXPERIENCE

Senior Product Manager — Acme Corp (Boston, MA)
Jan 2021 – Present
- Led the development and launch of a new enterprise search feature used by 200+ customers
- Managed a team of 6 engineers and 2 designers across the search and discovery workstream
- Worked with sales and customer success to gather requirements and prioritize the roadmap
- Shipped quarterly releases on time for 3 consecutive quarters
- Created and maintained product specs, PRDs, and roadmap documentation
- Collaborated with data science team on ML model improvements for search relevance

Product Manager — BuildBase (Cambridge, MA)
Jan 2019 – Dec 2020
- Built out the core workflow automation features from 0 to 1
- Ran weekly sprint planning and retrospectives with the engineering team
- Managed the customer feedback loop with 50+ enterprise accounts
- Launched integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, and Zapier
- Responsible for the product roadmap for the integrations workstream

Associate Product Manager — StartupCo (San Francisco, CA)
Jun 2017 – Dec 2018
- Supported senior PM on mobile app redesign
- Wrote user stories and acceptance criteria for engineering
- Conducted user interviews and synthesized findings into product specs
- Assisted with competitive analysis and market research

EDUCATION
B.S. Computer Science — University of Michigan, 2017

SKILLS
Product management, agile, scrum, Jira, Figma, SQL, Amplitude, user research, roadmapping, stakeholder management
---
evals.expected_output: A structured panel review with: (1) Recruiter score and feedback citing specific formatting/ATS/scan issues, (2) Hiring Manager score with specific citations of weak bullets and at least one rewritten example, (3) CPO score addressing narrative arc and career progression, (4) A scorecard table, (5) Top 5 prioritized edits, (6) Offer to rewrite sections.
expectations: Output includes scores from all three reviewers (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, CPO)
Each reviewer's feedback cites specific bullets or sections from the resume, not generic advice
The Hiring Manager section identifies outputs-vs-outcomes as a core issue
At least one specific bullet is rewritten in the output
A scorecard table is included with scores and top concerns
A top 5 prioritized edit list is provided
An offer to rewrite sections is made at the end

evals.prompt:
I want to evaluate my resume. I'm targeting Director of Product at AI companies — specifically companies doing AI infrastructure, developer tools, or LLM platforms (Cohere, Scale AI, Weights & Biases, Replicate). I have a strong background but I've been told my resume doesn't scream 'AI PM' even though I've been working on AI products for 3 years. No specific concerns beyond that — just give me the full review.

Here's my resume:

---
SAMANTHA CHEN
schen@proton.me | NYC (open to remote) | linkedin.com/in/samanthachen

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Product leader with 11 years of experience shipping data-intensive products at scale. Currently leading AI/ML product strategy at DataFlow. Proven track record of aligning engineering, data science, and go-to-market teams around shared product vision.

EXPERIENCE

Group Product Manager, AI Platform — DataFlow (New York, NY)
Mar 2022 – Present
- Own the roadmap for DataFlow's ML pipeline and model management products, serving 3,200 enterprise customers
- Reduced model deployment time from 3 weeks to 4 days by redesigning the CI/CD integration and shipping automated validation tooling
- Led a 3-team, 24-engineer organization through a platform rewrite that enabled DataFlow to support 10x data volume with no downtime SLA degradation
- Drove adoption of new AI features to 68% of the customer base within 6 months of launch through a tiered rollout and enablement program with customer success
- Partnered with GTM to define enterprise packaging and pricing for AI add-ons, contributing to $4.2M in incremental ARR
- Established the AI product council — a monthly cross-functional forum bringing together ML, data eng, security, and product to surface risk and align on model governance standards

Senior Product Manager, Data Products — DataFlow (New York, NY)
Mar 2020 – Feb 2022
- Launched real-time data pipeline product that processed 40B+ events/day at peak, reducing customer infrastructure costs by an average of 31%
- Grew the data products business from $8M to $22M ARR over 24 months
- Defined and shipped v1 of DataFlow's developer API, which became the primary integration surface for 60% of new enterprise accounts

Product Manager — Tensor Systems (San Francisco, CA)
Aug 2017 – Feb 2020
- Built and shipped the core ML experiment tracking product from 0 to 1, reaching 15,000 active researchers within 18 months of launch
- Led customer discovery with 80+ ML engineers and researchers to define the product roadmap
- Shipped 4 major releases per year on a consistent schedule across 3 years

Product Manager — Qubit Analytics (London, UK)
Sep 2014 – Jul 2017
- Owned the behavioral analytics and A/B testing product for mid-market e-commerce customers
- Grew the product from 120 to 380 paying customers over 3 years
- Launched a self-serve onboarding flow that reduced time-to-value from 45 days to 11 days

EDUCATION
MSc Computer Science — Imperial College London, 2014
BSc Mathematics — University of Edinburgh, 2013

SKILLS
Product strategy, ML/AI product development, platform architecture, data pipelines, API products, developer tools, cross-functional leadership, OKRs, product-led growth, Amplitude, Mixpanel, SQL, Python
---
evals.expected_output: A full panel review that specifically addresses the 'AI PM signal' concern. The CPO and Hiring Manager should diagnose why the resume doesn't read as AI-native despite real AI work, with specific suggestions for how to strengthen it. The recruiter should assess ATS keyword coverage for AI infrastructure/LLM platform roles.
expectations: Output includes scores from all three reviewers
The review directly addresses the user's stated concern about AI PM signal not coming through
The Hiring Manager or CPO identifies what specifically is missing to make the AI work read as more native/deep
The scorecard table is included
Top 5 prioritized edits are listed
The feedback is specific to this resume — not generic PM advice
Offer to rewrite sections is made

evals.prompt:
review my resume please, I'm a PM trying to move from mid-level to senior. targeting fintech companies, ideally Series B or C startups. I've had three PM jobs but none of them were at household-name companies so I'm worried I look 'small potatoes.'

---
ALEX TORRES
alex.torres@gmail.com | Chicago, IL | linkedin.com/in/alextorres

EXPERIENCE

Product Manager — PayCraft (Chicago, IL) | Apr 2022 – Present
PayCraft is a B2B payments platform for mid-market retailers.
- Owned the core checkout SDK used by 400 merchants processing $280M in annual payment volume
- Redesigned the merchant onboarding flow, cutting median time-to-first-transaction from 14 days to 3 days (78% reduction)
- Shipped fraud detection feature using ML signals that reduced false-positive rate by 34% without increasing chargebacks
- Led cross-functional team of 5 engineers, 1 designer, 1 data analyst on a 6-month platform migration
- Presented quarterly business reviews to the CFO and CTO on payments volume, fraud trends, and roadmap priorities

Product Manager — Kiosk (Remote) | Jan 2020 – Mar 2022
Kiosk is an inventory management tool for independent retailers.
- Launched iOS app from 0 to 1 that reached 8,000 monthly active users within 12 months
- Built and shipped a supplier integration marketplace connecting Kiosk users to 15 wholesale distributors
- Led end-to-end discovery for the invoicing product: 40+ user interviews, 3 prototypes, shipped in 4 months
- Maintained a 4.7 App Store rating over 18 months post-launch through rapid iteration on user feedback

Associate Product Manager — Voila Finance (Austin, TX) | Jun 2018 – Dec 2019
Voila Finance provided personal budgeting tools for Gen Z users.
- Supported launch of savings goal feature that was adopted by 62% of active users within 60 days
- Ran A/B tests across onboarding flow, improving D7 retention from 38% to 51%
- Managed relationships with 3 external API partners (Plaid, Stripe, MX)

EDUCATION
B.A. Economics — DePaul University, 2018

SKILLS
Product discovery, user research, A/B testing, fintech, payments, mobile (iOS/Android), Jira, Figma, Mixpanel, SQL, cross-functional teams
---
evals.expected_output: A panel review that addresses the user's concern about company name recognition. The reviewers should assess whether the work and metrics speak for themselves regardless of company brand, and give specific guidance on how to frame experience at lesser-known companies. The review should be direct about whether this resume reads as senior-ready.
expectations: Output includes scores from all three reviewers
The review directly engages with the 'small potatoes' concern — either validates it or pushes back with reasoning
The Recruiter gives a view on how unknown company names affect screening
The Hiring Manager assesses whether the metrics and scope convey senior-level work
A scorecard table is included
Top 5 prioritized edits are provided
At least one example of reframing or rewriting to maximize impact at unknown companies is included
Offer to rewrite sections is made
